name: runtime_project
description: Arcsi Runtime–based project — contains the full infrastructure context

topology.phone: Edge instance (Policy Layer) — Android phone, Termux, port 3000, Tailscale: YOUR_PHONE_TAILSCALE_IP
topology.proxmox: Core instance (Interpretation Layer) — Proxmox LXC, LAN: YOUR_PROXMOX_LAN_IP, Tailscale: YOUR_PROXMOX_TAILSCALE_IP

boundary_rules: Email logic, notification_rules.json — ALWAYS stays on the phone
Home Assistant, MQTT, qBittorrent — handled by Proxmox
LAN addresses (192.168.x.x) — only reachable through Proxmox

system_prompt: This is an Arcsi Runtime–based project. The system operates on a split‑brain model: Proxmox = Interpretation (WHAT IS THIS?), Phone = Policy (WHAT SHOULD I DO WITH IT?). The phone is where decisions live. Proxmox is where they execute. In every development step, keep the blueprint in mind: blueprints/arcsi_dataflow_graph_v0.1.png

memory_layers.development_memory: event_log.json + logProgress() — How did we get here?
memory_layers.lab_notebook: Every insight, decision, and dead end recorded

methodological_principles: Treat every obstacle as a design signal
Repeated pattern → elevate it into architecture (multiple times + independently)
First stabilize, then structure — never the other way around
The spiral: Test → Insight → Solution → Design → New perspective
